creator: matthieu.volat@gmail.com
text:
On Fri, 30 Dec 2011 11:53:50 -0800
Adrian Chadd <adrian@freebsd.org> wrote:

> Hi,
> 
> Are you able to try FreeBSD-7 with the AR9280, or maybe FreeBSD-8?
> 
> The multicast code should work; all kinds of strange things fail if
> multicast handling is broken.
> 
> Can you please compile the driver with "options ATH_DEBUG" and then
> enable mode change debugging:
> 
> sysctl dev.ath.X.debug=0x40
> 
> Then start your interface and report what's logged in dmesg.
> 

Hi and thanks for such a quick reply!

I don't have a ready 8-Current partition on this laptop (7 would not work I guess, support for the AR9280 came with 8.1/8.2), but I can upgrade if necessary (with FreeBSD 9 around the corner, I'm not to worried about destabilizing this setup).

Meanwhile, I compiler a generic + ATH_DEBUG kernel. More strange things begin to happen :
* A boot/for some time after wlan0 creation/setup, multicast (or at least, using the mdns addresses) do work, I was able to receive some with and without capture with tcpdump (who showed the capture).
* I left the capture going on, and tried periodicaly to make some mdns queries. After a few minutes, I was again in the situation described in the report.

I've attached the dmesg output from interface setting to the point I noticed the bug again.

Thanks again for taking time with that problem and happy new year ;)

-- 
Matthieu Volat <matthieu.volat@gmail.com>
